---
title: "Welcome to some of my data visualization projects"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
engine: knitr
---

# Review of Homelessness 2024

When looking at aggregate numbers state wide interesting patterns appear. All data is from the [Hud Exchange] and World Population News. <sup>[1][2]</sup>



### Individual Emergency Shelter Beds Per Homeless Individual
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# results: hide

packages <- c("tidyverse", "ggplot2", "plotly", "usmap")
invisible(lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
rm(packages)

HomeState <- read.csv("C:/Users/markg/OneDrive/Documents/Website/Data&Code/HomPopState.csv")

HomeStateUse <- HomeState |>
  mutate(total = rowSums(across(4:11), na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  mutate(BedPer = total/HPop,
         region = tolower(State))

states_map <- map_data("state")
states_map <- states_map |>
  left_join(HomeStateUse, by = "region")

ggplotly(
  ggplot(states_map, aes(
    x = long,
    y = lat,
    group = group,
    fill = BedPer,
    text = paste0(
      "State: ", tools::toTitleCase(region),
      "<br>Emergency Shelter Beds per Homeless Individual: ",
      round(BedPer, 2)
    )
  )) +
    geom_polygon(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +  # black outlines
    coord_fixed(1.3) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      name = "Emergency Shelter Beds per\nHomeless Individual",
      low = "#570C18",
      high = "#000087",
      midpoint = 1,
      labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Individual Emergency Shelter Beds per Homeless Individual by State (2024)",
      subtitle = "Red = shortage (< 1), Blue = surplus (> 1)"
    ) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right"
    ),
  tooltip = "text"
)
```
